Our recommendation was based in large part upon the requirements of federal law, specifically, those provisions of the Retirement Equity Act of 1984 defining a domestic relations order as a qualified domestic relations order if the order meets the requirements of Section 414(p) of the U.S. Internal Revenue Code. evidence_quotes.quote: FRS have the authority to divide a member's benefits and pay a portion of such divided benefits to a third party payee based on an order that expressly applies to a different retirement system? In response, it was the recommendation of this office that the domestic relations order clearly specify each plan to which such order applies.
